title: Fast-doubling Fibonacci in O(log n) = fib
statement_informal: The fast-doubling method computes Fibonacci numbers in O(log n) operations (vs O(n) naive) by recursing on the bits of n via fib(2k)=fib k*(2 fib(k+1)-fib k) and fib(2k+1)=fib k^2+fib(k+1)^2. fastpair n returns (fib n, fib(n+1)); correctness: fastpair fuel n = (fib n, fib (S n)) for n <= fuel.

cost_model.notes: Recurses on n/2 (~lg n levels), O(1) multiplications per level. Doubling identities derived from the addition formula fib(m+n+1)=fib(m+1)fib(n+1)+fib m fib n (two-step induction).

references: Fast-doubling Fibonacci (Dijkstra; the matrix-power / doubling identities). O(log n) vs the O(n) naive recurrence.
